name: Login Shell Overridden
notes:
This rule detects when a login shell gets overridden. Adversaries may override a login shell to achieve persistence.

Note: The Login Shell Filename reference set is pre-populated with Shell Filenames, and can be tuned.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Operating System
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Successful File Modification
when the event matches File Directory (custom) is any of [/usr/sbin or /bin or /usr/bin]
when any of Filename (custom) are contained in any of Login Shell Filename - AlphaNumeric
TD_list: APPLY Login Shell Overridden on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Operating System
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Successful File Modification
AND when the event matches File Directory (custom) is any of [/usr/sbin or /bin or /usr/bin]
AND when any of Filename (custom) are contained in any of Login Shell Filename - AlphaNumeric
RED_list: Event description: Detects when a login shell is overridden.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
